acceptance_criteria.text: ForgeLink has a central attention policy model covering SMS, agent channels, and trusted signals with defaults that avoid unnecessary interruption.
The policy supports quiet hours, urgency behavior, source/channel mute states, notification body redaction, and explicit overrides.
Desktop notifications use the policy and never include message bodies, phone numbers, secrets, personal media, or agent-human transcript content unless the human explicitly enables that behavior.
Settings UI lets the human inspect and adjust attention rules without hidden ranking or engagement controls.
Policy decisions are testable and covered by unit tests across SMS, agent messages, urgent approvals, RSS/signals, muted sources, quiet hours, and failure states.
Docs explain the attention model, privacy defaults, and remaining risks for local notifications and future mobile/remote bridges.
